skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image
skill.summary: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation wrapper for the inference.sh CLI tool. External commands (backticks in markdown) are code fences, not Ruby execution. Network URLs are legitimate documentation links to inference.sh platform. No cryptographic algorithms or malicious code present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Ruby Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backticks in markdown as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are actually markdown code fences for CLI command examples (e.g., infsh login, npx skills add). No Ruby code or shell execution present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed false positive - backticks are markdown syntax for code, the skill is pure documentation

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Legitimate Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hardcoded URLs as security concerns. URLs point to inference.sh platform documentation and cloud resources - expected for a CLI wrapper skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed false positive - all URLs are legitimate inference.sh service endpoints required for the skill to function

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with FLUX, Gemini, and 50+ Models
content.value_statement: Create professional AI images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and 50+ models directly from Claude or Codex via the inference.sh CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and 50+ AI models
Create text-to-image generations with customizable prompts
Use LoRA models for custom styles and fine-tuned outputs
Upscale and enhance existing images professionally
Render text accurately in images using Seedream and Reve models
Stitch multiple images together for compositions
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Network connection to inference.sh platform required
Image generation credits usage based on inference.sh pricing
Some advanced features require specific model subscriptions

use_cases.title: Create Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate product photos, social media graphics, and promotional materials using FLUX or Seedream models

use_cases.title: Generate Concept Art
use_cases.description: Create illustrations, concept art, and creative visuals for projects using various AI models

use_cases.title: Enhance Existing Images
use_cases.description: Upscale low-resolution images or remove backgrounds using specialized tools

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of {description} using FLUX model with {style} style.

prompt_templates.title: Product Photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of {product} with {lighting} lighting and {background} background.

prompt_templates.title: Creative Illustration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an artistic illustration of {subject} in {art_style} style with {mood} mood.

prompt_templates.title: Text Rendering
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image with the text '{text}' displayed in {font_style} font on a {background_type} background.

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a cat astronaut in space using FLUX
output_examples.output: An AI-generated image of a cat wearing a space suit floating in space with stars and planets in the background

output_examples.input: Create a product photo of a coffee mug with studio lighting
output_examples.output: Professional product photography of a ceramic coffee mug on a clean background with soft studio lighting

best_practices: Use specific, detailed prompts to get better results
Choose the right model for your use case (FLUX for quality, Gemini for speed)
Include composition and lighting details in prompts for better output
anti_patterns: Using overly vague prompts without specific details
Not specifying aspect ratio when needed for specific formats
Expecting perfect text rendering without using appropriate models like Seedream

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to sign up at inference.sh and install the CLI tool to use this skill.

faq.question: Which model should I use for text rendering?
faq.answer: Use Seedream 3.0 or Reve models for accurate text rendering in images.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires network access to the inference.sh platform for image generation.

faq.question: How do I ups existing images?
faq.answer: Use the Topaz Upscaler app via infsh app run falai/topaz-image-upscaler with the image_url parameter.

faq.question: What is the fastest model available?
faq.answer: Gemini 2.5 Flash offers the fastest generation speed among the available models.

faq.question: Can I create custom styles with LoRA?
faq.answer: Yes, FLUX Dev LoRA supports custom LoRA adapters for specific artistic styles.
